title: Everett Klippert arrested and later jailed indefinitely for being gay
description: Everett Klippert is arrested after telling police he is gay and does not intend to change. Two years later he is sentenced to an indefinite prison term, the last man in Canada imprisoned solely for homosexuality.

title: Stonewall riots in New York City
description: Patrons of the Stonewall Inn in Greenwich Village resist a police raid, sparking days of protest. The uprising is widely credited with launching the modern LGBTQ rights movement.

title: Canada decriminalizes homosexual acts between consenting adults
description: Canada removes criminal penalties for homosexual acts between consenting adults aged 21 and over. The reform is part of a broad amendment to the Criminal Code.

title: Everett Klippert released from prison
description: Everett Klippert is released from prison, two years after Canada decriminalized homosexual acts between consenting adults.

title: Quebec bans discrimination based on sexual orientation
description: The province of Quebec amends its Charter of Human Rights and Freedoms to prohibit discrimination based on sexual orientation, described as the first major jurisdiction in the world to do so.

title: Canada lifts immigration ban on homosexual men
description: Canada removes the provision barring homosexual men from immigrating to the country.

title: Toronto bathhouse raids spark mass protest
description: After police arrest more than 300 men in raids on four Toronto bathhouses, roughly 3,000 people march in protest. The demonstrations are often called Canada's Stonewall.

title: Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms guarantees equal protection
description: The Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms guarantees the right to equal protection and equal benefit of the law without discrimination. It does not name sexual orientation explicitly.

title: Supreme Court of Canada reads sexual orientation into the Charter
description: The Supreme Court of Canada rules that sexual orientation should be read into the equality guarantee of the Charter of Rights and Freedoms, extending its protection to lesbians and gay men.

title: Ontario adds sexual orientation to its Human Rights Code
description: Ontario becomes the second Canadian province to include sexual orientation as a protected class under its Human Rights Code.

title: Manitoba adds sexual orientation to its Human Rights Code
description: Manitoba becomes the third Canadian province to include sexual orientation as a protected class under its Human Rights Code.

title: Svend Robinson becomes Canada's first openly gay MP
description: Svend Robinson becomes the first openly gay Member of Parliament in Canada.

title: United Church of Canada allows ordination of gay and lesbian ministers
description: The United Church of Canada becomes the first church in the country to permit the ordination of gay and lesbian ministers.

title: Toronto proclaims its first official Pride Day
description: The City of Toronto officially proclaims Pride Day for the first time.

title: Canada lifts ban on gay and lesbian service members
description: Canada removes the prohibition on homosexuals serving in the Canadian Forces.

title: Libby Davies becomes Canada's first openly lesbian MP
description: Libby Davies becomes the first openly lesbian Member of Parliament in Canada.

title: British Columbia and Ontario begin marrying same-sex couples
description: Court rulings clear the way for British Columbia and Ontario to begin issuing marriage licences to same-sex couples, ahead of nationwide recognition.

title: Canada legalizes same-sex marriage nationwide
description: Canada officially sanctions same-sex marriage across the country, becoming the fourth nation in the world to do so.
